gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/221)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/221)

parts.text: عَبْدِ الرَّحْمَنِ بْنِ زَيْدِ بْنِ أَسْلَمَ

text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) إِذَا دَخَلْتُمْ بِلَاداً فَكُلُوا مِنْ بَصَلِهَا يَطْرُدْ عَنْكُمْ وَبَاءَهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad Bin Khalid, from Muhammad Bin Ali, from Abdul Rahman Bin Zayd Bin Aslam,
(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘When you enter a city, so eat from its onions, it would dispel from you its plague’.
en.sarwar: 5. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad ibn Khalid from Muhammad ibn Ahmad from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Zayd ibn Aslam who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said that the Messenger of Allah, O Allah, grant compensation to Muhammad and his family worthy of their services to Your cause, has said, ‘When you enter a town eat its onion, it keeps plague away from you.’”
